summary: Matched several previously stubbed grCorneria stage functions and tightened Corneria ground variable layout/prototypes. The PR renamed two anonymous generator callbacks in symbols/header from fn_801E2454/fn_801E2480 to grCorneria_801E2454/grCorneria_801E2480, implemented position helper functions, state-reset functions, a stage-ID check for Corneria/Venom, and an accessor for Corneria xD0. The key structural fix was changing grCorneria_GroundVars bitfield groups xC4 and xC6 into unions with both named bit access and whole-byte value access, enabling matching code that zeroes or assigns the entire flag byte.
what_changed: Implemented grCorneria_801DDCF0(Vec3*) to fetch ground object 3 via Ground_801C2BA4(3), compute offset-relative x/y using gv.corneria.offset_x, offset_y.val, xE4, and xE8, and set z to 0.0f.
Implemented grCorneria_801DDD4C(Vec3*) to fetch ground object 3 and compute base-plus-offset x/y from gv.corneria.base_x, base_y, offset_x, and offset_y.val.
Reworked Corneria flag accesses from xC4_flags/xC6_flags to union fields xC4.flags, xC4.value, xC6.flags, and xC6.value.

Simplified grCorneria_801DFC2C reset logic by using GET_GROUND(gobj), clearing xC6.value, clearing offset_y.flags.b0, memzeroing 0x18 bytes starting at xC8, clearing xC4.value, and setting gp->x11_flags.b012.
Implemented grCorneria_801E0140 with the same animation/reset pattern seen in grCorneria_801DFEB8: clear xC6.flags.b0, call grAnime_801C8138, clear xC4.value, and set x11_flags.b012.
Implemented grCorneria_801E0F34(Ground_GObj*, int) to clear xCC/xC8, clamp the integer argument to the range 0..0x13, and store it into the xC4 byte.
Implemented grCorneria_801E2454(Vec3*) and grCorneria_801E2480(Vec3*) as grLib_801C96F8 generator wrappers using IDs 0x7534 and 0x7530 with count/parameter 0x1E.
Implemented grCorneria_801E2CE8(void) to return true when stage_info.internal_stage_id is CORNERIA or VENOM.
Implemented grCorneria_801E2EA0(void) to return gv.corneria.xD0 from ground object 3 when available, otherwise 0.0f.
Updated grcorneria.h prototypes for newly typed functions, including Vec3*, Ground_GObj*, int, HSD_Generator*, and f32 return types.
Renamed config symbols fn_801E2454 and fn_801E2480 to grCorneria_801E2454 and grCorneria_801E2480.
Updated includes in grcorneria.c to use forward headers and explicit gr/ include paths, adding gr/grlib.h for grLib_801C96F8.
smart_moves: Used unions for xC4 and xC6 so the code can match both individual bitfield operations and whole-byte stores/clears without losing readable flag names.

Replaced a sequence of individual field clears in grCorneria_801DFC2C with memzero(&gp->gv.corneria.xC8, 0x18), matching the observed contiguous reset pattern and reducing manual field-by-field noise.
Promoted anonymous local function names to stage-scoped grCorneria_* names once their role as Corneria generator callbacks was understood, keeping symbols aligned with neighboring stage functions.
Typed small helper functions in the public header as soon as their signatures were known, replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with Vec3*, Ground_GObj*, int, HSD_Generator*, and f32.
Reused existing ground access and stage globals rather than inventing abstractions: Ground_801C2BA4(3), GET_GROUND, stage_info.internal_stage_id, CORNERIA, and VENOM.
decomp_lessons: When a ground variable byte is sometimes addressed as named bits and sometimes as an integer byte, represent it as a union of a bitfield struct and a value member; this can unlock matches while preserving semantic access.
Contiguous struct clears in assembly may be better represented as memzero over a known field range than as many scalar assignments, especially inside ground variable reset functions.
Stage helper functions that only read/write a Vec3 often fetch a canonical ground object by map/index and compute positions directly from stage-specific gv fields.
If a previously anonymous fn_* callback is passed to a stage-specific generator setup, it may deserve a grStage_address name and a typed prototype.
Header cleanup is part of matching work: replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with exact types improves callsite readability and helps future matching.
Stage checks can include sibling or reused stages; in this slice Corneria logic explicitly treats both CORNERIA and VENOM as true.
naming_conventions: Stage-local matched functions use the grCorneria_80xxxxxx naming pattern rather than generic fn_80xxxxxx once associated with Corneria.
Bitfield unions were named by offset-like field names xC4 and xC6, with nested .flags for bit access and .value for whole-byte access.
Vector output parameters in prototypes were named pos or Vec3* where the function writes a position.
The project prefers f32 in headers for float-returning decomp prototypes when matching surrounding typedef conventions.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added PAD_STACK(4) in the Vec3 helper functions, indicating stack-padding was needed for matching those small functions.
Used memzero(&gp->gv.corneria.xC8, 0x18) to match a block clear spanning xC8 and following Corneria ground variable fields.
Stored a clamped integer into xC4 via *(int*)(&gp->gv.corneria.xC4) in grCorneria_801E0F34, suggesting a matching-motivated cast/store pattern rather than a purely type-safe byte assignment.
Changed flag writes from xC4_flags.b0/xC4_flags.b1 to xC4.flags.b0/xC4.flags.b1 and whole-byte clears to xC4.value = 0 to match different access widths.
Used GET_GROUND(gobj) in places where the diff indicates it produced the desired code shape compared with direct gobj->user_data access.

review_feedback.author: YunataSavior
review_feedback.interpretation: No technical review feedback was present in the provided slice; this appears to be a progress/milestone comment.

follow_up_queries: Look for other grCorneria functions still using UNK_RET or fn_* names around 801E24AC, 801E2D14, 801E2D90, and 801E2E50.
Search other stage GroundVars structs for bitfield-only bytes that may need union { flags; value; } treatment to match whole-byte stores.

Compare grCorneria_801E2454 and grCorneria_801E2480 callsites and generated code, especially because the diff excerpt shows grCorneria_801E2480 calling grLib_801C96F8 without an explicit return despite an HSD_Generator* prototype.
Review remaining Corneria reset/init functions for opportunities to replace scalar clears with memzero when contiguous struct ranges are cleared in assembly.
